originPortalInfo.title: Museum of Finnish Architecture
originPortalInfo.description: An architectural museum in Helsinki, Finland. Established in 1956, it is the second oldest museum in the world devoted specifically to architecture. The museum was founded on the basis of the photographic collection of the Finnish Association of Architects (SAFA), which was established in 1949. Since 1981 the museum is housed in a neo-classical building, designed by architect Magnus Schjerfbeck and completed in 1899. The building was originally in the use of a scientific society and the University of Helsinki.

originPortalInfo.title: Helsinki Design Museum
originPortalInfo.description: A museum in Helsinki devoted to the exhibition of both Finnish and foreign design, including industrial design, fashion, and graphic design. The museum, first founded in 1873, is one of the oldest in the world, but has operated in its present premises, a former school, designed by architect Gustaf Nyström in 1894 in the neo-Gothic style, since 1978.

destinationPortalInfo.description: An architectural museum in Helsinki, Finland. Established in 1956, it is the second oldest museum in the world devoted specifically to architecture. The museum was founded on the basis of the photographic collection of the Finnish Association of Architects (SAFA), which was established in 1949. Since 1981 the museum is housed in a neo-classical building, designed by architect Magnus Schjerfbeck and completed in 1899. The building was originally in the use of a scientific society and the University of Helsinki.

originPortalInfo.title: Erottajan Paviljonki
originPortalInfo.description: Arkkitehti: Alvar Aalto, 1951. Helsingin kaupunki järjesti vuonna 1941 arkkitehtuurikilpailun Erottajan mäen alle rakennettavan väestönsuojan ympäristön järjestelyistä, jonka Aallon suunnitelma voitti. Sotatilan vuoksi alkuperäinen suunnitelma jäi toteuttamatta, ja paikalle suunniteltiin ja rakennettiin aluksi vain väliaikaiseksi tarkoitettu puinen rakennelma portaineen. Lopulta nykyinen, väestönsuojan sisäänkäynniksi suunniteltu ”paviljonki”, rakennettiin vasta vuonna 1951.
